Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Nov-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Steven M. Gevercer, Esq., appeared for Defendant & moving party: Jeanne Woodford, Chairperson Corrections Standard Authority
Daniel B. Swerdlin, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & opposing party: Candace Waters
Ruling:
Tentative Ruling adopted: Defendant James Tilton's Motion For Protective Order is granted.
The depositions are irrelevant and are barred as they are sought to obtain the perspectives, insights, and reasoning for Board members' decisions, and their individual processes of making suitability determinations. The allegations of the complaint are that the Corrections Standards Authority has failed to act in compliance with the law. The reasons the agency allowed the alleged failures in the suitability determination process and any disconnect between regulation, policy, and practice, as relevant, are an organizational act and fall with the organization's knowledge. A Person Most Knowledgeable deposition of the CSA is the proper means of obtaining that knowledge.
The board members acted in a quasi-judicial manner in making a determination of specific rights under existing law with regard to a specific fact situation. Discovery of mental processes are not discoverable. See William S. Hart Union High School District v Regional Planning Committee, 176 Cal.App.3d 1009.
As to both motions, all requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
